basics.description:
## 🛡️ SpyCloud Sentinel Supreme

![SpyCloud](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL/main/docs/images/SpyCloud-Logo-white.png)

**Transform recaptured darknet data into automated identity threat protection.**

This wizard deploys the complete SpyCloud integration including:
- CCF Data Connector with 5 REST API pollers
- 6 custom log tables (118+ columns)
- 5 Logic App playbooks (MDE, CA, Credential, Blocklist, TI Enrichment)
- 43+ analytics rules + Fusion + 5 Microsoft Security Incident Creation rules
- UEBA, Anomaly Detection, EyesOn audit settings
- 3 automation rules (auto-triage, device tasks, auto-close)
- 15 hunting queries across all MITRE ATT&CK tactics
- 4 watchlists (VIP, IOC Blocklist, Approved Domains, High-Value Assets)
- Security Copilot plugin + interactive agent (58+ skills)
- Comprehensive connector + feature recommendations

**Prerequisite:** SpyCloud Enterprise API key from [portal.spycloud.com](https://portal.spycloud.com)

[Documentation](https://github.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL) · [Architecture](https://github.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL/blob/main/docs/architecture.md)

options.text: This wizard guides you through every configuration option. Required fields are marked with *. All playbooks and analytics rules are optional and can be toggled individually.

**Region Notes:** Microsoft Sentinel is available in all listed regions. Some features (UEBA behavioral baselines, Fusion ML) require 14+ days of data collection regardless of region. Logic Apps are available in all regions. If a feature is unavailable in your selected region, the template will skip it gracefully without failing the deployment.

Choose how to deploy SpyCloud Sentinel. **Full Deployment** installs all selected components. **Upgrade Existing** adds new features to an existing deployment without touching existing resources. **Selective modes** deploy only specific components.

All modes are safe to re-run — existing resources are not modified unless explicitly selected.

elements.name: deploymentMode
elements.label: Deployment Mode
elements.toolTip: Select deployment mode based on your needs. Full is recommended for first-time installations.

allowedValues.label: Full Deployment (first-time install — all selected features)
Upgrade Existing (add new features, preserve existing resources)
Connector Only (data pipeline — DCE, DCR, tables, connector)

elements.defaultValue: Full Deployment (first-time install — all selected features)

options.text: In Azure, the Log Analytics workspace and Sentinel workspace are the SAME resource. Microsoft Sentinel is a solution installed ON TOP of a Log Analytics workspace. When you select or create a workspace here, ALL SpyCloud resources will be deployed into that workspace.

elements.name: createNewWorkspace
elements.label: Workspace Mode
elements.defaultValue: Use existing workspace (Sentinel must be enabled)
elements.toolTip: Create a new workspace or use an existing one.

allowedValues.label: Use existing workspace (Sentinel must be enabled)

elements.name: existingWorkspaceSelector
elements.label: Select Existing Workspace
elements.toolTip: Select an existing Log Analytics workspace that has Microsoft Sentinel enabled.

elements.name: newWorkspaceName
elements.label: New Workspace Name
elements.toolTip: Name for the new workspace. Must be 3-63 characters.

constraints.validationMessage: 3-63 chars: letters, numbers, hyphens. Must start with a letter.

elements.name: retentionInDays
elements.label: Data Retention (days)
elements.toolTip: How long to retain data. 90 days included free. Longer retention incurs additional cost.

options.text: All analytics rule categories are **ENABLED by default** with recommended settings. Rules that require data sources you haven't connected will auto-skip gracefully. You can disable individual rules post-deployment in Sentinel > Analytics. Uncheck categories below only if you explicitly don't want them.

elements.name: enableCoreDetectionRules
elements.label: Deploy Core Detection Rules (12 rules)
elements.toolTip: Fundamental SpyCloud detection rules. These work with SpyCloud data alone — no additional connectors required.

options.text:
**12 Rules — No additional connectors required:**

| ID | Name | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| sc-001 | Infostealer Credential Exposure (sev >= 20) | High |
| sc-002 | Plaintext Password Exposure | High |
| sc-003 | Session Cookie Theft / MFA Bypass (sev 25) | High |
| sc-004 | PII / SSN / National ID Exposure | High |
| sc-005 | Executive / VIP User Exposure | High |
| sc-006 | Multi-Domain Credential Reuse (3+ domains) | Medium |
| sc-007 | Device Reinfection Pattern (2+ infections) | High |
| sc-008 | High-Sighting Credential (>3 sources) | Medium |
| sc-009 | New Malware Family Targeting Org | Medium |
| sc-010 | Stale Exposure Without Remediation (>48h) | Medium |
| sc-011 | Corporate Email on Consumer Site | Medium |
| sc-012 | Exposure Volume Spike (anomaly) | Medium |

**Data Sources:** SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL, SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL
**Watchlists:** SpyCloud-VIP-Watchlist, SpyCloud-Approved-Domains

elements.name: sectionO365Rules
elements.label: O365 & Entra ID Correlation (sc-020 to sc-029)

elements.name: enableO365EntraRules
elements.label: Deploy O365 & Entra ID Correlation Rules (10 rules) — Recommended
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Cross-correlates SpyCloud exposures with Entra ID sign-ins, Office 365 activity, impossible travel, mailbox rules, OAuth consent, and admin role changes. Rules auto-skip if data sources aren't connected.

options.text:
**10 Rules — Requires: Microsoft Entra ID + Office 365 connectors**

| ID | Name | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| sc-020 | Exposed Credential + Successful Sign-in | High |
| sc-021 | Exposed User + Risky Sign-in (Identity Protection) | High |
| sc-022 | Exposed User + Impossible Travel | High |
| sc-023 | Exposed User MFA Registration Change | High |
| sc-024 | Exposed User Mailbox Rule Creation (BEC) | High |
| sc-025 | Exposed User OAuth App Consent | High |
| sc-026 | Exposed User Admin Role Assignment | High |
| sc-027 | Exposed User Self-Service Password Change | Medium |
| sc-028 | Exposed User eDiscovery Export | High |
| sc-029 | Exposed User SharePoint Mass Download | High |

**Required Data Sources:** SigninLogs, AuditLogs, OfficeActivity, AADUserRiskEvents
**Setup:** Install 'Microsoft Entra ID' + 'Office 365' connectors from Content Hub
**Entra ID:** Portal > Entra ID > Diagnostic Settings > Send SigninLogs + AuditLogs to workspace
**Office 365:** Sentinel > Data Connectors > Office 365 > Enable Exchange + SharePoint + Teams

elements.name: enableUebaFirewallRules
elements.label: Deploy UEBA & Firewall Correlation Rules (10 rules) — Recommended
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Correlates SpyCloud with UEBA behavioral anomalies, firewall logs, DNS queries, and VPN sessions. Detects lateral movement, C2 communication, and anomalous access. Rules auto-skip if data sources aren't connected.

options.text:
**10 Rules — Requires: UEBA enabled + Firewall connector(s)**

| ID | Name | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| sc-030 | UEBA Anomalous Behavior from Exposed User | High |
| sc-031 | First-Time Access from Exposed User | Medium |
| sc-032 | Firewall Deny from SpyCloud Infected IP | Medium |
| sc-033 | Fortinet FSSO Session from Exposed User | High |
| sc-034 | PaloAlto User-ID Traffic from Exposed User | High |
| sc-035 | DNS C2 Communication from Infected Device | High |
| sc-036 | VPN New Location from Exposed User | Medium |
| sc-037 | Firewall Allow After Credential Exposure | High |
| sc-038 | MDE Infected Machine Active on Network | High |
| sc-039 | Lateral Movement from Compromised Account | High |

**Required Data Sources:** BehaviorAnalytics (UEBA), CommonSecurityLog (firewalls), DnsEvents
**Setup:** Enable UEBA in Sentinel Settings. Install firewall connector (Fortinet/PaloAlto/Check Point) from Content Hub.

elements.name: enableAdvancedThreatRules
elements.label: Deploy Advanced Threat Detection Rules (10 rules) — Recommended
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Detects multi-stage attacks, CA bypass, credential re-exposure after reset, TI indicator matches, and geographic mismatches. Uses multiple data sources with graceful fallback.

options.text:
**10 Rules — Requires: Multiple data sources (varies per rule)**

| ID | Name | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| sc-040 | Multi-Stage Attack (exposure + sign-in + lateral) | High |
| sc-041 | Conditional Access Bypass from Exposed User | High |
| sc-042 | User Re-Exposed After Password Reset | High |
| sc-043 | TI Indicator Match on SpyCloud IP | High |
| sc-044 | Azure Firewall + SpyCloud IP Correlation | Medium |
| sc-045 | Sensitive Source Breach Detection | High |
| sc-046 | First-Time Domain Access from Exposed User | Medium |
| sc-047 | Credential Theft Tool Detection (Mimikatz, etc.) | High |
| sc-048 | Geographic Mismatch (exposure vs sign-in country) | High |
| sc-049 | Check Point + SpyCloud IP Correlation | Medium |

**Required Data Sources:** Varies — SigninLogs, CommonSecurityLog, ThreatIntelligenceIndicator, AzureNetworkAnalytics_CL
**Note:** Rules auto-skip gracefully if required data source is not connected.

elements.name: enableMsicRules
elements.label: Deploy MSIC Rules (auto-create incidents from Microsoft alerts)
elements.toolTip: Creates Sentinel incidents from Defender XDR, Entra ID Protection, Defender for Cloud, MCAS, and Defender for Identity alerts.

options.text:
**5 Rules — Auto-create incidents from Microsoft security products:**

1. **Defender XDR** — Endpoint, Identity, Office 365, Cloud Apps alerts
2. **Entra ID Protection** — Risky users, risky sign-ins (requires Entra P2)
3. **Defender for Cloud** — VM, SQL, Storage, K8s, App Service alerts
4. **Cloud App Security** — Impossible travel, OAuth abuse, mass downloads
5. **Defender for Identity** — Pass-the-hash, golden ticket, DCSync

Each rule requires its corresponding connector. Rules deploy but produce no incidents if the connector isn't installed.

elements.name: enableFusionDetection
elements.label: Enable Fusion Advanced ML Detection
elements.toolTip: Patented ML that correlates low-fidelity alerts across kill chain stages. Detects credential theft → lateral movement → exfiltration chains.

options.text:
Each playbook is a Logic App with a **system-assigned managed identity**. After deployment, you MUST grant API permissions to each playbook's managed identity.

**Post-Deployment Steps (for ALL playbooks):**
1. Azure Portal > Resource Group > select Logic App
2. Click 'Identity' in left menu > 'System assigned' tab > copy Object ID
3. Go to 'Enterprise Applications' > search by Object ID > 'Permissions' > 'Grant admin consent'
4. Or run: `scripts/grant-permissions.sh` from Cloud Shell

**Required RBAC on the resource group:**
- Logic App Contributor
- Managed Identity Operator
- Microsoft Sentinel Automation Contributor

elements.name: enableForcePasswordResetPlaybook
elements.label: SpyCloud-ForcePasswordReset (Recommended)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Forces password change on next sign-in via Microsoft Graph API. User must re-authenticate with MFA. Critical for any infostealer credential exposure response.

options.text:
**SpyCloud-ForcePasswordReset**
- **Action:** PATCH /users/{id} — sets forceChangePasswordNextSignInWithMfa = true
- **API Permissions:** User.ReadWrite.All (Microsoft Graph)
- **License:** Entra ID P1+ (included in M365 E3/E5)
- **Post-Deploy:** Grant admin consent for User.ReadWrite.All to the Logic App managed identity
  ```
  az ad app permission grant --id <managed-identity-object-id> --api 00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000 --scope User.ReadWrite.All
  ```

elements.name: enableRevokeSessionsPlaybook
elements.label: SpyCloud-RevokeSessions (Recommended)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Instantly revokes ALL active sessions, refresh tokens, and browser cookies for compromised users. Essential when session cookies are stolen.

options.text:
**SpyCloud-RevokeSessions**
- **Action:** POST /users/{id}/revokeSignInSessions — invalidates all refresh tokens and session cookies
- **API Permissions:** User.ReadWrite.All (Microsoft Graph)
- **License:** Entra ID P1+ 
- **Post-Deploy:** Same as ForcePasswordReset — grant User.ReadWrite.All

elements.name: enableEnforceMfaPlaybook
elements.label: SpyCloud-EnforceMFA (Recommended)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Deletes compromised MFA methods (phone, authenticator app) and forces secure re-registration. Prevents attackers from using stolen MFA tokens.

options.text:
**SpyCloud-EnforceMFA**
- **Actions:** DELETE /users/{id}/authentication/phoneMethods/{id}, DELETE /users/{id}/authentication/softwareOathMethods/{id}, then adds user to MFA re-registration group
- **API Permissions:** UserAuthenticationMethod.ReadWrite.All, GroupMember.ReadWrite.All (Microsoft Graph)
- **License:** Entra ID P1+ (P2 for risk-based re-registration)
- **Warning:** This will delete ALL existing MFA registrations. Users must re-register from scratch.
- **Post-Deploy:** Grant admin consent for UserAuthenticationMethod.ReadWrite.All AND GroupMember.ReadWrite.All

elements.name: enableBlockConditionalAccessPlaybook
elements.label: SpyCloud-BlockConditionalAccess (Recommended)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Adds compromised users to severity-tiered CA security groups: Sev 25 → Block All, Sev 20 → Require Compliant Device, Sev 5+ → Require MFA.

options.text:
**SpyCloud-BlockConditionalAccess**
- **Actions:** Adds user to severity-appropriate security group:
  - Severity 25 (session cookies) → SpyCloud-Blocked group (block all access)
  - Severity 20 (infostealer) → SpyCloud-Restricted group (require compliant device + MFA)
  - Severity 5+ (breach) → SpyCloud-Monitored group (require MFA from untrusted locations)
- **API Permissions:** GroupMember.ReadWrite.All, Directory.Read.All (Microsoft Graph)
- **License:** Entra ID P1+ (for Conditional Access policies)
- **Pre-Requisite:** Create the 3 security groups in Entra ID and configure CA policies targeting them BEFORE deployment
- **Post-Deploy:** Grant admin consent for GroupMember.ReadWrite.All and Directory.Read.All

elements.name: enableBlockFirewallPlaybook
elements.label: SpyCloud-BlockFirewall (Recommended)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Auto-pushes malicious IP block rules to Fortinet/PaloAlto/Azure Firewall from SpyCloud C2 data. Configure firewall API keys post-deploy.

options.text:
**SpyCloud-BlockFirewall**
- **Actions:** Creates address objects on FortiGate and/or PAN-OS for each malicious IP
- **Fortinet:** POST /api/v2/cmdb/firewall/address (requires FortiGate REST API token)
- **Palo Alto:** POST /api/?type=config&action=set (requires PAN-OS API key)
- **API Permissions:** None (uses firewall API keys, not Azure managed identity)
- **License:** None (requires firewall admin access)
- **Pre-Requisite:** Generate API keys on your firewall management console
- **Post-Deploy:** Update Logic App parameters with firewall API URLs and keys

elements.name: enableIsolateDevicePlaybook
elements.label: SpyCloud-IsolateDevice (Recommended)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Isolates infected devices via MDE API. Sev 25 = full isolation, Sev 20 = selective (allows Outlook/Teams). Tags device in MDE portal.

options.text:
**SpyCloud-IsolateDevice**
- **Actions:**
  - Severity 25 → Full isolation (device completely disconnected except MDE agent)
  - Severity 20 → Selective isolation (limited connectivity maintained)
  - Looks up device by hostname from SpyCloud infected_machine_id
- **API Permissions:** Machine.Isolate, Machine.ReadWrite.All (WindowsDefenderATP API)
- **License:** Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2 (~$5.20/user/month, included in M365 E5)
- **Post-Deploy:** Grant API permissions:
  1. Enterprise Applications > search for Logic App name
  2. Add permission for 'WindowsDefenderATP' > Machine.Isolate + Machine.ReadWrite.All
  3. Grant admin consent

elements.name: enableNotifyUserPlaybook
elements.label: SpyCloud-NotifyUser (Recommended)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Emails exposed users with breach details, required actions (change password, review sign-ins), and IT support contacts.

options.text:
**SpyCloud-NotifyUser**
- **Action:** Sends Office 365 email to the affected user with: exposure summary, required actions (change password, review sign-ins), support contact
- **API Permissions:** Mail.Send (Microsoft Graph)
- **License:** Exchange Online (included in M365)
- **Post-Deploy:** Grant Mail.Send permission. Configure 'From' address (shared mailbox recommended).

elements.name: enableNotifySOCPlaybook
elements.label: SpyCloud-NotifySOC (Recommended)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Posts Adaptive Cards to SOC Teams channel with severity, affected users, recommended response actions, and direct incident link.

options.text:
**SpyCloud-NotifySOC**
- **Action:** Posts Adaptive Card to Teams channel via webhook with: severity, affected users, exposure type, recommended remediation actions, direct link to incident
- **API Permissions:** None (uses Teams incoming webhook URL)
- **License:** Microsoft Teams (included in M365)
- **Post-Deploy:** Create incoming webhook in Teams channel and update Logic App parameter with the URL

elements.name: enableEnrichIncidentPlaybook
elements.label: SpyCloud-EnrichIncident (Recommended)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Auto-enriches every incident with SpyCloud context: breach source, exposure count, password reuse analysis, malware family, and device forensics.

options.text:
**SpyCloud-EnrichIncident**
- **Action:** Queries SpyCloud data for incident entities, adds detailed comments to the incident, applies tags (e.g., 'malware-sourced', 'plaintext-password'), adjusts incident severity based on SpyCloud severity score
- **API Permissions:** Microsoft Sentinel Responder role (on the workspace)
- **License:** None (included with Sentinel)
- **Post-Deploy:** Assign 'Microsoft Sentinel Responder' role to the Logic App managed identity on the workspace

elements.name: enableFullRemediationPlaybook
elements.label: SpyCloud-FullRemediation (Recommended - chains all playbooks)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Master orchestrator: Phase 1 (parallel): password reset + session revocation + device isolation → Phase 2: MFA enforcement + CA group → Phase 3: SOC notification. Best for severity 25 incidents.

options.text:
**SpyCloud-FullRemediation**
- **Phase 1 (parallel):** Force password reset + Revoke sessions + Isolate device
- **Phase 2 (parallel):** Enforce MFA + Assign CA group
- **Phase 3:** Notify SOC via Teams
- **Requires:** ALL permissions from the individual playbooks above
- **License:** All licenses required by the individual playbooks
- **Post-Deploy:** Grant ALL permissions listed for each individual playbook to this single Logic App's managed identity
- **Recommendation:** Use this as the single automation rule target for severity 25 incidents
These playbooks were part of the original SpyCloud deployment template. They remain available for backward compatibility.

elements.name: enableMdePlaybook
elements.label: Playbook: MDE Device Isolation (Recommended)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Isolates compromised devices via MDE API based on SpyCloud severity. Requires: MDE P2.

elements.name: enableCaPlaybook
elements.label: Playbook: Conditional Access Identity Protection (Recommended)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Adds compromised users to CA security groups for identity protection. Requires: Entra ID P1/P2.

elements.name: enableCredResponsePlaybook
elements.label: Playbook: Credential Response + Teams Alert (Recommended)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Password reset + Teams SOC alert in a single playbook. Combines identity remediation with SOC awareness.

elements.name: enableMdeBlocklistPlaybook
elements.label: Playbook: MDE Blocklist Scheduled Scan (Recommended)
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Scheduled scan that auto-isolates devices matching severity 25 infostealer records. Runs every 4 hours by default.

elements.name: enableTiEnrichmentPlaybook
elements.label: Playbook: TI Enrichment (VirusTotal + AbuseIPDB) — Recommended
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Enriches incidents with VirusTotal, AbuseIPDB, and GreyNoise TI lookups. Free API keys supported. Deploys but skips lookups until API keys are configured.

elements.name: virusTotalApiKey
elements.toolTip: Get free key at virustotal.com/gui/my-apikey

options.text:
Create these security groups in Entra ID BEFORE deployment, then enter their Object IDs below.

| Group | Purpose | CA Policy |
|---|---|---|
| SpyCloud-Blocked | Severity 25 | Block all access |
| SpyCloud-Restricted | Severity 20 | Require compliant device + MFA |
| SpyCloud-Monitored | Severity 5+ | Require MFA from untrusted locations |

**To create:** Entra ID > Groups > New Group > Security > enter name > Create > copy Object ID

elements.name: blockedGroupId
elements.label: SpyCloud-Blocked Group Object ID
elements.toolTip: Object ID for the block-all-access group.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid GUID or leave blank.

elements.name: restrictedGroupId
elements.label: SpyCloud-Restricted Group Object ID
elements.toolTip: Object ID for the restricted-access group.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid GUID or leave blank.

elements.name: monitoredGroupId
elements.label: SpyCloud-Monitored Group Object ID
elements.toolTip: Object ID for the monitored group.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid GUID or leave blank.

elements.name: caSecurityGroupId
elements.label: Legacy CA Security Group Object ID (optional)
elements.toolTip: For backward compat with original CA playbook.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid GUID or leave blank.

options.text:
Configure ticketing system integrations to automatically create tickets/work items for credential exposures. Each system supports custom field mappings, priority mapping, and templates.

**Severity to Priority Mapping:**
| SpyCloud Severity | Priority |
|---|---|
| 25 (session cookies) | Critical / P1 / Highest |
| 20 (infostealer) | High / P2 / High |
| 5 (breach) | Medium / P3 / Medium |
| 2 (low) | Low / P4 / Low |

elements.name: enableServiceNow
elements.label: Enable ServiceNow ticket creation
elements.toolTip: Automatically creates incidents/security incidents in ServiceNow for SpyCloud exposures.

options.text:
**ServiceNow Setup:**
1. Create an integration user in ServiceNow with 'itil' role
2. For OAuth: Admin > System OAuth > Application Registry > Create new
3. For REST API: System Web Services > REST API Explorer to test
4. The Logic App will POST to /api/now/table/{table_name}

**Required ServiceNow Roles:** itil, security_incident_writer (for sn_si_incident table)

elements.name: serviceNowInstanceUrl
elements.label: ServiceNow Instance URL
elements.toolTip: Your ServiceNow instance URL.

elements.name: serviceNowAuthType
elements.label: Authentication Type
elements.toolTip: How to authenticate with ServiceNow.

elements.name: serviceNowUsername
elements.label: Username
elements.toolTip: ServiceNow integration user.

elements.name: serviceNowPassword
elements.toolTip: ServiceNow user password.

elements.name: serviceNowTable
elements.label: Target Table
elements.toolTip: Which ServiceNow table to create records in.

elements.name: serviceNowAssignmentGroup
elements.label: Assignment Group
elements.toolTip: ServiceNow assignment group for created tickets.

elements.name: serviceNowCategory
elements.label: Category
elements.toolTip: Ticket category.

elements.name: serviceNowShortDescriptionTemplate
elements.label: Short Description Template
elements.toolTip: Template for ticket short description. Use {severity}, {email}, {severity_label}.

elements.name: serviceNowCustomFields
elements.label: Custom Fields (JSON)
elements.toolTip: Additional custom fields as JSON key-value pairs.

elements.name: enableJira
elements.label: Enable Jira issue creation
elements.toolTip: Automatically creates Jira issues for SpyCloud exposures.

options.text:
**Jira Setup:**
1. Create an API token: id.atlassian.com > Security > API tokens > Create
2. Or use Automation webhook: Project Settings > Automation > Create rule > Incoming webhook
3. The Logic App will POST to /rest/api/3/issue

**Required Jira Permissions:** Create issues in the target project, Browse projects

elements.name: jiraInstanceUrl
elements.label: Jira Instance URL
elements.toolTip: Your Jira Cloud or Server instance URL.

elements.name: jiraAuthType
elements.label: Authentication Type
elements.toolTip: How to authenticate with Jira.

elements.name: jiraEmail
elements.label: Jira Email
elements.toolTip: Email associated with the API token.

elements.name: jiraApiToken
elements.toolTip: Jira API token or PAT.

elements.name: jiraProjectKey
elements.label: Project Key
elements.toolTip: Jira project key where issues will be created (e.g., SEC, SECOPS, SOC).

elements.name: jiraIssueType
elements.label: Issue Type
elements.toolTip: Jira issue type for created tickets.

elements.name: jiraLabels
elements.label: Labels (comma-separated)
elements.toolTip: Labels to apply to created issues.

elements.name: jiraSummaryTemplate
elements.label: Summary Template
elements.toolTip: Template for issue summary. Use {severity}, {email}, {severity_label}.

elements.name: jiraCustomFields
elements.label: Custom Fields (JSON)
elements.toolTip: Additional custom fields as JSON.

elements.name: enableAzureDevOps
elements.label: Enable Azure DevOps work item creation
elements.toolTip: Automatically creates work items in Azure DevOps for SpyCloud exposures.

options.text:
**Azure DevOps Setup:**
1. Create a Personal Access Token: dev.azure.com > User Settings > PAT > New Token
2. Scopes needed: Work Items (Read & Write)
3. Or use Service Hook: Project Settings > Service Hooks > Create
4. The Logic App will POST to /_apis/wit/workitems/${{type}}

**Required Permissions:** Work Items > Read & Write in the target project

elements.name: azureDevOpsOrgUrl
elements.label: Organization URL
elements.toolTip: Your Azure DevOps organization URL.

elements.name: azureDevOpsProject
elements.label: Project Name
elements.toolTip: Target project for work items.

elements.name: azureDevOpsWorkItemType
elements.label: Work Item Type
elements.toolTip: Work item type to create.

elements.name: azureDevOpsAreaPath
elements.label: Area Path (optional)
elements.toolTip: Area path for work item categorization.

elements.name: azureDevOpsIterationPath
elements.label: Iteration Path (optional)
elements.toolTip: Iteration/sprint path.

elements.name: azureDevOpsAssignedTo
elements.label: Assigned To (optional)
elements.toolTip: Default assignee email for work items.

elements.name: azureDevOpsTags
elements.label: Tags (semicolon-separated)
elements.toolTip: Tags applied to work items.

elements.name: azureDevOpsTitleTemplate
elements.label: Title Template
elements.toolTip: Template for work item title. Use {severity}, {email}, {severity_label}.

options.text: Configure notification channels for SpyCloud alerts. Each channel supports severity filtering and message customization. Multiple channels can be active simultaneously.

elements.name: enableSlackNotifications
elements.label: Enable Slack notifications
elements.toolTip: Send SpyCloud alerts to a Slack channel via incoming webhook.

options.text:
**Slack Setup:**
1. Go to api.slack.com/apps > Create New App > From scratch
2. Features > Incoming Webhooks > Activate
3. Add New Webhook to Workspace > select channel > Authorize
4. Copy the webhook URL

Or: Slack channel > Settings > Integrations > Apps > Add Incoming Webhook

elements.name: slackWebhookUrl
elements.label: Slack Webhook URL
elements.toolTip: Incoming webhook URL for the target Slack channel.

elements.name: slackChannel
elements.label: Channel Override (optional)
elements.toolTip: Override the default channel configured in the webhook.

elements.name: slackBotName
elements.label: Bot Display Name
elements.toolTip: Name shown as the message sender in Slack.

elements.name: slackMessageFormat
elements.label: Message Format
elements.toolTip: Message format style.

allowedValues.label: Rich (Block Kit — interactive buttons, formatted sections)

elements.defaultValue: Rich (Block Kit — interactive buttons, formatted sections)

elements.name: slackSeverityFilter
elements.label: Severity Filter
elements.toolTip: Filter which alerts are sent to Slack.

elements.name: slackIncludeUserDetails
elements.label: Include user details (email, domain) in messages
elements.toolTip: WARNING: This sends PII (email addresses) to Slack. Ensure your Slack workspace has appropriate data handling policies.

elements.name: enableTeamsNotifications
elements.label: Enable Microsoft Teams notifications
elements.toolTip: Send SpyCloud alerts to a Teams channel via incoming webhook.

options.text:
**Teams Setup:**
1. Teams > select channel > Manage channel (or '...' menu)
2. Connectors (or Workflows) > Incoming Webhook > Configure
3. Give it a name (e.g., 'SpyCloud Alerts') > Create
4. Copy the webhook URL

**Note:** Microsoft is migrating from Connectors to Workflows. If 'Incoming Webhook' connector is not available, use Power Automate Workflows > 'Post to a channel when a webhook request is received'.

elements.name: teamsChannelWebhook
elements.label: Teams Webhook URL
elements.toolTip: Incoming webhook URL for the SOC Teams channel.

elements.name: teamsChannelName
elements.label: Channel Name (for display)
elements.toolTip: Display name used in playbook comments and logs.

elements.name: teamsCardFormat
elements.label: Card Format
elements.toolTip: Message card format.

elements.name: teamsSeverityFilter
elements.label: Severity Filter
elements.toolTip: Filter which alerts are sent to Teams.

elements.name: teamsIncludeRemediationActions
elements.label: Include remediation action buttons in cards
elements.toolTip: Adds clickable action buttons (View Incident, Reset Password, Isolate Device) to Teams cards. Requires Power Automate or Logic App HTTP triggers.

elements.name: enableEmailNotifications
elements.label: Enable email notifications
elements.toolTip: Send SpyCloud alerts via Office 365 email.

options.text:
**Email Setup:**
- The playbook's managed identity needs Mail.Send permission on Microsoft Graph
- Recommended: Use a shared mailbox (e.g., spycloud-alerts@company.com) as the sender
- For distribution lists: Add the DL as a recipient in 'To' or 'CC'

**Post-Deploy:** Grant Mail.Send permission to the Logic App managed identity

elements.name: emailFromAddress
elements.label: From Address
elements.toolTip: Sender address. Use a shared mailbox for best practice.

elements.name: emailToAddresses
elements.label: To Addresses (comma-separated)
elements.toolTip: Primary recipients.

elements.name: emailCcAddresses
elements.label: CC Addresses (comma-separated, optional)
elements.toolTip: CC recipients.

elements.name: emailSubjectTemplate
elements.label: Subject Template
elements.toolTip: Email subject template. Use {severity}, {email}, {severity_label}.

elements.name: emailIncludeBreachDetails
elements.label: Include breach details (source, malware family, sighting count)
elements.toolTip: Adds detailed breach metadata to the email body.

elements.name: emailIncludeRecommendedActions
elements.label: Include recommended actions checklist
elements.toolTip: Adds a step-by-step remediation checklist to the email.

elements.name: enableNotifications
elements.label: Enable ingestion health alerts
elements.toolTip: Creates an Action Group that alerts when SpyCloud data ingestion stops for 3+ hours.

elements.name: notificationEmail
elements.label: Health Alert Email
elements.toolTip: Email for infrastructure health alerts.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid email or leave blank.

elements.name: teamsWebhookUrl
elements.label: Teams Webhook for Health Alerts (optional)
elements.toolTip: Separate webhook for infrastructure alerts (different from SOC webhook above).

options.text:
These settings enable core Sentinel platform features. All are included in Microsoft Sentinel at no additional cost.

- **UEBA:** Creates behavioral baselines for users. Required for sc-030 (UEBA Anomaly) rule.
- **Anomaly Detection:** ML-powered deviation detection. Auto-tunes over 14 days.
- **Fusion:** Correlates low-fidelity alerts into high-confidence multistage attack incidents.

**Required role:** Security Administrator on the workspace

elements.name: enableUEBA
elements.label: Enable UEBA (User and Entity Behavior Analytics)
elements.toolTip: Deploys EntityAnalytics, UEBA, and EyesOn settings.

elements.name: enableAnomalies
elements.label: Enable Anomaly Detection (ML-powered)
elements.toolTip: Enables built-in ML anomaly rules.

elements.name: enableFusionRule
elements.label: Enable Fusion Multistage Attack Detection
elements.toolTip: WARNING: Set to false if Fusion is already enabled in your workspace. Can only install once per workspace.

elements.name: enableMicrosoftSecurityIncidentRules
elements.label: Create MSIC Rules (4 rules)
elements.toolTip: Auto-create incidents from Entra ID Protection, Defender for Cloud, MCAS, and Defender for Identity alerts.

elements.name: enableCompass
elements.label: Enable Compass Connectors (Enterprise+ only)
elements.toolTip: Enable SpyCloud Compass consumer/partner identity exposure connectors. Requires Enterprise+ API subscription. Leave unchecked if you only have Enterprise tier.

elements.name: enableWorkbook
elements.label: Deploy SpyCloud Executive Dashboard (8-tab workbook) — Recommended
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Rich executive dashboard with risk scores, exposure trends, breach intelligence, remediation metrics, and compliance reporting.

elements.name: enableSocWorkbook
elements.label: Deploy SpyCloud SOC Operations Dashboard (4-tab workbook) — Recommended
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Operational dashboard for SOC analysts: live threat feed, investigation hub, correlation matrix, hunting queries, and automation metrics.

options.text: Workbooks are accessible from Sentinel > Workbooks after deployment. No additional configuration needed.

elements.name: enableSessionCookieDetection
elements.label: Enable session cookie / token theft detection — Recommended
elements.toolTip: RECOMMENDED — Detects severity 25 exposures where stolen session cookies enable MFA bypass. Most critical detection category.

elements.name: enableIdentityProviderAlerts
elements.label: Enable IdP correlation rules (Okta/Duo/Ping/Entra)
elements.toolTip: Cross-references SpyCloud with IdP sign-in logs.

elements.name: enableAnalyticsRule
elements.label: Create primary infostealer detection rule
elements.toolTip: The main rule that detects sev 20+ exposures and generates incidents.

elements.name: enableAutomationRule
elements.label: Create automation rule (auto-trigger playbooks)
elements.toolTip: Fires enabled playbooks when SpyCloud incidents are created.

elements.name: enablePostDeployScript
elements.label: Run post-deployment script (resolve DCE/DCR + assign RBAC)
elements.toolTip: Automates DCE/DCR resolution and RBAC assignment.

elements.name: tagEnvironment
elements.label: Environment
elements.toolTip: Environment classification.

elements.name: tagOwner
elements.label: Owner
elements.toolTip: Team or person responsible.

elements.name: tagCostCenter
elements.label: Cost Center
elements.toolTip: Cost center for billing.

options.text:
**VALIDATE BEFORE DEPLOYING:**

**1. SpyCloud API Key**
- Obtain from portal.spycloud.com > API Keys
- Test: `curl -H 'X-Api-Key: YOUR_KEY' https://api.spycloud.io/enterprise-v2/breach/catalog/1`
- Enterprise tier = Watchlist + Catalog | Enterprise+ = adds Compass

**2. Azure RBAC Roles (on resource group)**
- Microsoft Sentinel Contributor
- Log Analytics Contributor
- Logic App Contributor (if deploying playbooks)
- Security Administrator (if enabling UEBA)

**3. Network Access**
- Outbound HTTPS (443) to api.spycloud.io
- No inbound rules required

**4. Post-Deployment Permissions (CRITICAL)**
After deployment, each playbook's managed identity needs API permissions:
```
# Run in Cloud Shell:
scripts/grant-permissions.sh
```
Or manually:
- Enterprise Applications > find Logic App > Permissions > Grant admin consent
- Assign: User.ReadWrite.All, Machine.Isolate, Mail.Send (per playbook)

**5. Verification Commands**
```
# Check roles:
az role assignment list --assignee $(az ad signed-in-user show --query id -o tsv) --query '[].roleDefinitionName' -o tsv

# Check Sentinel:
az sentinel onboarding-state show --resource-group $RG --workspace-name $WS

# Check data flow (after deployment):
# Log Analytics > Logs > SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | take 10
```

**No data after 15 min:** Test API key with curl. Check outbound HTTPS to api.spycloud.io.
**401 Unauthorized:** Wrong key type (need Enterprise tier).
**Compass tables empty:** Requires Enterprise+ subscription.
**Data stops after initial sync:** Check DCR metrics in Azure Monitor. May be rate-limited.
**Duplicate records:** Normal during initial sync — deduplicate with `| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by document_id`
**High costs:** Filter to severity >= 20, increase polling interval, reduce retention.

**Playbooks not triggering:** Check: 1) Analytics rule enabled? 2) Automation rule enabled? 3) Logic App enabled?
**403 Forbidden:** Managed identity missing permissions. Run scripts/grant-permissions.sh.
**MDE isolation fails:** Verify MDE P2 license and device onboarding.
**Password reset fails:** Verify Entra P1+ and User.ReadWrite.All permission.
**MFA deletion fails:** Need UserAuthenticationMethod.ReadWrite.All.
**Teams not sending:** Regenerate webhook URL in Teams channel.

**Debug:** Resource Group > Logic Apps > select playbook > Run History > expand failed actions

**Rule shows 'Error':** Open > Edit > Run query manually in Log Analytics.
**No incidents despite data:** Lower triggerThreshold or expand queryPeriod.
**Too many incidents:** Increase threshold, add severity filters, group by entity.
**Cross-source rules empty:** Install required connector from Content Hub.
**UEBA rules empty:** Enable UEBA + wait 14 days for baselines.
**Fusion not detecting:** Enable Fusion + need multiple signal sources.

Run in Log Analytics:

**1. Health Check:**
```
union
  (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Watchlist'),
  (SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Catalog')
| project Table, Records, Latest
```

**2. Playbook Status:**
```
SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL | union Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize Total=count(), Success=countif(ActionStatus=='Success'), Failed=countif(ActionStatus=='Failed') by Action
```

**3. Remediation Gap:**
```
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(48h) and severity >= 20 | distinct email;
let remediated = SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(48h) | distinct Email;
exposed | where email !in (remediated)
```
